name: Petronet LNG
oneLine: 0/6 hardening headers despite strong email auth; ADFS exposed in CT
findings: All 6 hardening headers missing (HTTP 405 response on root)
ADFS endpoint visible in CT logs
Strong email-auth posture (SPF strict, DMARC p=reject)

fingerprints.stack: Application server returning HTTP 405 on root; ADFS endpoint adfs.petronetlng.com; SMTP infra (smtpdahej, smtpdelhi)
versions: ADFS version not exposed; no Server header disclosed
eolFlags: All 6 hardening headers MISSING on root response

attackPaths.name: ADFS metadata exposure → SAML token forgery / NTLM relay
attackPaths.entry: ADFS endpoint at adfs.petronetlng.com publicly named in CT; metadata at /FederationMetadata/2007-06/FederationMetadata.xml may be unauthenticated.
attackPaths.pivot: Harvest signing certificates and token endpoints; craft forged SAML/OIDC tokens or initiate NTLM relay (CVE-2025-33073).
attackPaths.objective: Unauthorised access to Petronet systems; impersonate employees; SAP / ERP access.

attackPaths.name: NTLM relay targeting ADFS-backed Windows infrastructure
attackPaths.entry: Hybrid Azure AD with NTLM auth typical for corporate; attacker on network or via compromised app.
attackPaths.pivot: Capture NTLM exchange; relay to ADFS or internal service without SMB signing.
attackPaths.objective: Privilege escalation; MFA bypass if NTLM is primary factor.

attackPaths.name: SMTP recon + sector-themed phishing
attackPaths.entry: smtpdahej / smtpdelhi named in DNS; SPF strict / DMARC reject blocks direct spoofing but enables intel.
attackPaths.pivot: Craft convincing internal-looking emails to Petronet employees with SideCopy-style oil-and-gas regulatory lures.
attackPaths.objective: Initial compromise via email malware (CurlBack / Spark / Xeno RAT).

attackPaths.name: Missing hardening headers → clickjacking + reflected XSS
attackPaths.entry: 0/6 hardening headers; HTTP 405 on root suggests app server with no header middleware.
attackPaths.pivot: Frame login pages in iframe overlay; reflected XSS via user-input parameters.

paths.preAi: ADFS metadata + NTLM relay → admin token forgery → SAP access
paths.mythos: Energy-company ADFS becomes attacker's authentication oracle; impersonate CFO; access LNG shipment schedules, pricing, supply agreements; pivot to OT via SAP-connected SCADA
paths.factor: ~10–20× (ADFS = crown jewel of Microsoft identity stack; LNG pricing/logistics is strategic IP)

paths.preAi: SMTP recon + crafted phishing → credential harvest before relay attempted
paths.mythos: Phishing low-cost; high ROI if employee training weak

cisoPatchList.title: Restrict ADFS metadata endpoint; require authentication
cisoPatchList.fix: PowerShell: Set-AdfsProperties -AllowAnonymousAccess $false. IIS URL Rewrite: block /FederationMetadata unless from whitelisted partner federation IPs.

cisoPatchList.title: Enforce MFA on all ADFS-backed accounts; disable legacy NTLM
cisoPatchList.fix: Azure AD Conditional Access → MFA required all users. ADFS auth providers → Forms + Microsoft Identity. Group Policy → 'Restrict NTLM: Incoming NTLM traffic' → Deny all.
cisoPatchList.validation: Login without MFA rejected; NTLM auth attempts in event log show denials

cisoPatchList.title: Patch Windows DCs for CVE-2025-33073; enforce SMB signing
cisoPatchList.fix: Apply current Microsoft Security Update on all DCs. Group Policy SMB signing → Required.

cisoPatchList.title: Add all 6 hardening headers to petronetlng.com
cisoPatchList.fix: HSTS, CSP, X-Frame-Options DENY, X-Content-Type-Options nosniff, Referrer-Policy strict-origin-when-cross-origin, Permissions-Policy.

cisoPatchList.title: Document and harden SMTP infra (smtpdahej, smtpdelhi)
cisoPatchList.fix: Restrict SMTP relay to authenticated internal users. Enforce SMTP TLS. nmap on 25/465/587 should show filtered or auth-required.
cisoPatchList.validation: External SMTP test rejected; mxtoolbox shows no open relay

cisoPatchList.title: TLS renewal scheduled for July 2026 (ahead of 2026-09-12 expiry)
cisoPatchList.fix: Calendar alert 2026-07-01. Generate CSR; submit to GoDaddy or alternate CA.

phase2.headlineQuestion: Is the ADFS metadata endpoint at adfs.petronetlng.com publicly accessible without authentication, and is MFA enforced on all user accounts?
phase2.coverageNotes: ADFS endpoint DNS-resolvable but HTTP not actively probed. SMTP server IPs not resolved (possibly network-restricted). Internal AD versions/patches unverifiable from external recon.
